title: 6.2.1 Water Cycle
description: Water cycle (hydrologic cycle) — every fact from the Grade 10 Biology textbook.

questions.question: Is water on earth stationary?
options: Yes, it stays in one place
No, it is always on the move
questions.correctAnswer: No, it is always on the move

questions.question: Rain that falls at a particular place can be the result of evaporation that occurred where?
options: At a distant ocean days before
questions.correctAnswer: At a distant ocean days before

questions.question: Water in a river may have come from which sources?
options: Only from rainfall at the same location
Rainfall at a distance or melting ice or snow from high mountains
questions.correctAnswer: Rainfall at a distance or melting ice or snow from high mountains

questions.question: What cycle is responsible for water movement in the ocean, on the land, and in the atmosphere?
Does the earth have a limited or unlimited amount of water?
What percentage of water on Earth is non-potable saltwater?
Of the remaining water after saltwater, what percentage is locked underground as groundwater or as icecap in polar areas?
What percentage of freshwater on Earth is easily accessible from lakes and rivers?
What is the water cycle also known as?
Between which components does water continuously cycle?
options: The oceans, atmosphere, land, and organisms
questions.correctAnswer: The oceans, atmosphere, land, and organisms

questions.question: What is the main driving force in the water cycle?
options: A change in temperature, heating, and cooling
questions.correctAnswer: A change in temperature, heating, and cooling

questions.question: What causes a change in the state of water in the water cycle?
What are the three states of water involved in the water cycle?
options: Water vapour (gas), ice or snow (solid), and liquid water
questions.correctAnswer: Water vapour (gas), ice or snow (solid), and liquid water

questions.question: What are the most important processes in the water cycle?
options: Evaporation, transpiration, condensation, precipitation, and runoff
questions.correctAnswer: Evaporation, transpiration, condensation, precipitation, and runoff

questions.question: The water cycle is driven by which energy source?
What helps the water cycle to start with evaporation?
From where does evaporation of water occur in the water cycle?
options: From the earth and the surface of leaves (transpiration)
questions.correctAnswer: From the earth and the surface of leaves (transpiration)

questions.question: What form does water take when it rises into the atmosphere?
What happens to water vapor as it rises and cools in the atmosphere?
options: It condenses into rain or snow
questions.correctAnswer: It condenses into rain or snow

questions.question: When water vapor condenses and falls to the earth's surface, what is this process called?
What forms of precipitation are mentioned in the water cycle description?
After precipitation, where does surface runoff water collect?
options: In rivers, lakes, soil, and soft rocks
questions.correctAnswer: In rivers, lakes, soil, and soft rocks

questions.question: What happens to some of the water that falls on earth?
options: It is taken up by plant roots and much of it flows into oceans or infiltrates the soil as groundwater
It all stays on the surface
questions.correctAnswer: It is taken up by plant roots and much of it flows into oceans or infiltrates the soil as groundwater

questions.question: How can water accumulated in the ground return to the surface?
options: As a spring or stream, or by being pumped out
questions.correctAnswer: As a spring or stream, or by being pumped out

questions.question: For what purposes can groundwater be pumped out?
How does pumped groundwater rejoin the water cycle?
What process involves water evaporating from the surface of leaves?
What is the term for the combined evaporation from the earth and transpiration from plants?
In the water cycle experiment with beakers, what process causes water particles to move up towards the plastic cover?
In the water cycle experiment, when water drops become too heavy and fall back to the small beaker, what process is demonstrated?
